file.content:
import type { Project } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "hivemind",
  code: "HVM",
  title: "Hivemind",
  tagline: "Strategic analytical software.",
  kind: "company",
  status: "active",
  startedISO: "2024-06-01",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-12",
  stage: "Seed",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/hivemind",
  // TODO(michael): supply real links
  links: [
    { label: "hivemind.ai",            href: "https://hivemind.ai",            external: true },
    { label: "app.hivemind.ai",        href: "https://app.hivemind.ai",        external: true },
    { label: "michael@hivemind.ai",    href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai" },
  ],
  summary:
    "Hivemind is strategic analytical software. It decomposes strategy problems into evaluable hypotheses with weighted evidence and probabilistic aggregation. The output is a structured brief, not a chat transcript.",
};

export default project;

import type { Project } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "purposeless-efficiency",
  code: "PRP",
  title: "Purposeless Efficiency",
  tagline: "Book I — corporatism, gamification, incumbency, complacency, economic revolution.",
  kind: "book",
  status: "draft",
  startedISO: "2024-01-15",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-12",
  stage: "manuscript · MS v4",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/purposeless-efficiency",
  links: [
    { label: "manuscript page", href: "/purposeless-efficiency" },
    { label: "notify list",     href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai?subject=PRP%20%2F%20NOTIFY" },
  ],
  summary:
    "The first volume of a five-book series. Argues that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and traces its consequences for work, governance, and the citizen.",
  citation: "Quintin, M. (forthcoming). Purposeless Efficiency. Book I of a five-volume series.",
};

export default project;

file.content:
import type { Project } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "theseus",
  code: "THS",
  title: "Theseus",
  tagline: "A knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction.",
  kind: "research",
  status: "active",
  startedISO: "2024-09-01",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-19",
  stage: "live",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/theseus",
  links: [
    { label: "thesescodex.com", href: "https://thesescodex.com", external: true },
    { label: "michael@hivemind.ai", href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai" },
  ],
  summary:
    "Theseus extracts first principles from discussion and writing, represents them as a graph, and uses a model called Noosphere to surface contradictions and ask refining questions.",
};

export default project;

content.content:
1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline sourced from src/content/projects/hivemind/thesis.ts.
4	// Framing composed from thesis.ts (mechanism), capabilities.ts (CAP list),
5	// workflow.ts (six-step pipeline), and the layered scoring contract in
6	// src/lib/coherenceEngine/arguments.ts (CE2 §2-§4).
7	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
8	  slug: "hivemind",
9	  code: "HVM",
10	  title: "Hivemind",
11	  tagline: "Strategic analytical software.",
12	  status: "in-progress",
13	  framing:
14	    "Hivemind decomposes strategy questions into evaluable hypotheses, attaches weighted evidence, and aggregates probabilistic posteriors into a structured brief. The output is a structured memo with citations and confidence intervals, not a chat transcript. The bet is that decision-grade analysis benefits from explicit structure scored against a layered coherence contract.",
15	  externalLinks: [
16	    { label: "hivemind.ai", href: "https://hivemind.ai" },
17	    { label: "app.hivemind.ai", href: "https://app.hivemind.ai" },
18	  ],
19	  seedQuestion: "what does Hivemind actually do?",
20	};
21	
22	export default metadata;
23

1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline + framing sourced from existing repo content:
4	//   - preface.ts / preface.mdx → severance of efficiency from purpose,
5	//     metric-as-meaning, "you have already noticed the symptoms"
6	//   - pillars.ts (I–V)        → corporatism, gamification, incumbency,
7	//                                complacency, economic revolution
8	//   - arc.ts                  → Book I of a five-volume series
9	//   - quotes.ts               → "Efficiency, severed from purpose, becomes
10	//                                the most efficient way of arriving nowhere."
11	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
12	  slug: "purposeless-efficiency",
13	  code: "PRP",
14	  title: "Purposeless Efficiency",
15	  tagline: "Book I — efficiency severed from purpose.",
16	  status: "in-progress",
17	  framing:
18	    "Purposeless Efficiency is a book-length argument that the late industrial era has been reorganized around optimization without an examined end — efficiency severed from purpose, the metric become the meaning. Book I of a five-volume series, it traces the consequences across five pillars: corporatism, gamification, incumbency, complacency, and the shape of an economic revolution that does not repeat the failure modes of the last one.",
19	  externalLinks: [],
20	  seedQuestion: "what is purposeless efficiency?",
21	};
22	
23	export default metadata;
24

1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline + framing sourced from existing repo content:
4	//   - thesis.ts      → claim ("philosophical debate can be systematized"),
5	//                       method (extract first principles, graph supports/
6	//                       tensions, surface contradictions), role
7	//                       ("a Kim/Codex for the operator-philosopher")
8	//   - methodology.ts → extraction with provenance, Noosphere walking the
9	//                       graph, slow structured collaborator (not a chatbot)
10	//   - principles.ts  → the seed dataset (P-001..P-080) with explicit
11	//                       supports / tensions edges
12	// The intended src/lib/theseus/* files (ordersDeck.ts, thesisAxes.ts,
13	// sources.ts) referenced by the prompt do not currently exist in the repo;
14	// the framing therefore stays grounded in the content/ files above.
15	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
16	  slug: "theseus",
17	  code: "THS",
18	  title: "Theseus",
19	  tagline:
20	    "A knowledge system for monitoring contradiction in the principles you have already committed to.",
21	  status: "shipped",
22	  framing:
23	    "Theseus extracts first principles from discussion and writing, links them into a graph of supports and tensions, and runs a second model — Noosphere — across the graph to flag contradictions, ask refining questions, and propose edits. The product is a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves. The output is a contradiction map with provenance, not a debate.",
24	  externalLinks: [
25	    { label: "thesescodex.com", href: "https://thesescodex.com" },
26	  ],
27	  seedQuestion: "what does the Theseus project argue about identity?",
28	};
29	
30	export default metadata;
31

1	// TODO(michael): rewrite in your voice
2	const thesis = {
3	  thesis:
4	    "Hivemind is strategic analytical software for operators who must reason under uncertainty.",
5	  mechanism:
6	    "It decomposes strategy questions into evaluable hypotheses, attaches weighted evidence, and aggregates probabilistic posteriors into a structured brief.",
7	  moat:
8	    "The graph of hypotheses, evidence, and counterfactuals compounds with use. The longer an operator works in Hivemind, the harder the work is to redo elsewhere.",
9	};
10	export default thesis;
11

1	// Prose narrative for the HVM project page.
2	// Sources:
3	//   - thesis.ts        → product thesis, mechanism, moat
4	//   - capabilities.ts  → CAP-01..CAP-06 capability ladder
5	//   - workflow.ts      → six-step pipeline (intake → render brief)
6	//   - lib/coherenceEngine/arguments.ts → S1..S5 layer weights, anti-gaming
7	//   - lib/coherenceEngine/pitches.ts   → domain-relative comparator
8	// Per v2 principle 2 (content first), each section opens with its point.
9	
10	export type BodySection = {
11	  slug: string;
12	  heading: string;
13	  paragraphs: string[];
14	};
15	
16	const sections: BodySection[] = [
17	  {
18	    slug: "what-is-hivemind",
19	    heading: "What is Hivemind",
20	    paragraphs: [
21	      "Hivemind is strategic analytical software for operators who must reason under uncertainty. It decomposes a strategy question into a tree of evaluable sub-claims, attaches weighted evidence to each branch, and aggregates probabilistic posteriors back to the root. The output is a structured brief — citations, intervals, dissents — not a chat transcript.",
22	      "The thesis is plain. Decision-grade analysis benefits from explicit structure rather than freeform prose. A memo that hides its assumptions is one nobody can argue with. A graph that lays them out is one a team can attack, defend, and update without redoing the work.",
23	    ],
24	  },
25	  {
26	    slug: "the-product",
27	    heading: "The product",
28	    paragraphs: [
29	      "The working surface is a hypothesis tree. An operator drops a strategy question in plain prose; Hivemind parses for entities and decisions and returns an initial frame. From there the workflow runs six steps: intake, decompose, gather evidence, score, red team, render brief. Each step has its own artifact — frame, tree, ledger, posterior, critique, memo — and each is auditable independently of the others.",
30	      "Capabilities currently shipping include hypothesis decomposition (CAP-01) and the evidence ledger (CAP-02), where sourced citations are weighted by provenance and recency. Scenario trees (CAP-03) and counterfactual simulation (CAP-04) are in alpha: branch probability over outcomes, hold inputs constant, vary one assumption at a time. Adversarial review (CAP-05) and the briefing render (CAP-06) are scoped and not yet shipping.",
31	      "The graph compounds. The longer an operator works inside Hivemind, the harder the working knowledge is to redo elsewhere; the moat is the accumulated structure, not the model behind it.",
32	    ],
33	  },
34	  {
35	    slug: "why-coherence-matters",
36	    heading: "Why coherence matters",
37	    paragraphs: [
38	      "Hivemind sits on a coherence engine. Coherence here is not “does the text sound confident” — it is a layered, falsifiable score over a structured argument. Five layers contribute under fixed weights: contradiction (S1, 0.30), argumentation (S2, 0.20), embedding similarity (S3, 0.20), compression (S4, 0.15), and structural connectivity (S5, 0.15). Each layer returns a number a human can inspect: which proposition pairs were flagged, the size of the grounded extension, whether a fixed-point iteration found a cycle, the longest support chain.",
39	      "Anti-gaming is a separate term. Template overlap, prior-corpus echo, contradiction denial, repetitive filler, and fluency-without-content are weighted and subtracted from a clamped floor. The composite is the layered score multiplied by the anti-gaming score; the brief that sounds smartest is not the brief that wins.",
40	      "The product layer above this is comparative. A pitch is scored against the median coherence of incumbents in its primary domain. The interesting number is domain-relative: a pitch can be coherent in absolute terms and still under-perform the incumbents in its own market. Operators making a real bet should know which one they are looking at.",
41	    ],
42	  },
43	  {
44	    slug: "what-it-isnt",
45	    heading: "What it isn’t",
46	    paragraphs: [
47	      "Hivemind is not a chat assistant with citations bolted on. The primitive is the hypothesis, not the message. There is no transcript to scroll; there is a graph to walk, and every node is independently rebuttable.",
48	      "It is not a forecasting market. Posteriors come from evidence and structure, not from crowd betting; the surface that matters is the one a single operator can defend in writing.",
49	      "It is not a single model. The coherence engine combines a contradiction-detection backend, a graph extension procedure, an embedding similarity check, a compression ratio, and a structural connectivity score. Swapping any one of those backends does not change the contract the engine makes with its caller.",
50	    ],
51	  },
52	  {
53	    slug: "status",
54	    heading: "Status",
55	    paragraphs: [
56	      "Hivemind is in active development; the company is at seed stage as of 2026-04. Hypothesis decomposition, the evidence ledger, and the workflow scaffold are usable end-to-end. Scenario branching and counterfactual simulation are in alpha against a small set of operator partners. Adversarial review and the briefing render are scoped and not yet shipping. A recorded walkthrough is pending.",
57	    ],
58	  },
59	];
60	
61	export default sections;
62

1	// TODO(michael): replace with the real preface
2	const preface = [
3	  "This book began as a refusal. The refusal was simple: to keep producing inside systems whose only consistent output was the perpetuation of themselves. The refusal was also, more uncomfortably, a confession. I was good at producing inside those systems. The confession is what made the book necessary.",
4	  "Purposeless Efficiency is the first of five volumes. It examines what efficiency means once it has been severed from any external purpose — once the metric is the meaning. The remaining volumes will treat what comes after the severance: the institutions that survive it, the ones that do not, and what an honest economic order might look like once we admit the mistake.",
5	  "Reader, I assume you have already noticed the symptoms. The book is for the reader who has stopped pretending the symptoms are accidental.",
6	];
7	export default preface;
8

1	// TODO(michael): rewrite each pillar body in your voice
2	export type Pillar = {
3	  num: "I" | "II" | "III" | "IV" | "V";
4	  title: string;
5	  seed: string;       // single-line collapsed quote
6	  body: string;       // expanded paragraph(s)
7	};
8	
9	const pillars: Pillar[] = [
10	  { num: "I",  title: "Corporatism",
11	    seed: "What it has done to work, and what it calls 'ambition.'",
12	    body: "Corporatism is the management of human attention by institutions whose primary product is no longer goods or services but the legitimation of their own continued existence. Ambition, under corporatism, is the willingness to mistake the institution's survival for one's own." },
13	  { num: "II", title: "Gamification",
14	    seed: "The industry of scoring, ranking, grinding; and its psychological residue.",
15	    body: "When every workflow becomes a leaderboard, the leaderboard becomes the workflow. The optimization target eats the optimized object. Gamification is not a UX choice; it is a worldview." },
16	  { num: "III", title: "Incumbency",
17	    seed: "Why the machine does not die when it should.",
18	    body: "Incumbents persist not because they are right but because they are wired into the substrate of decision-making. Their replacement requires not better arguments but a different substrate." },
19	  { num: "IV", title: "Complacency",
20	    seed: "The citizen's withdrawal, and the elite's.",
21	    body: "Complacency is the rational response to a system whose levers have been disconnected from outcomes. The cost of paying attention exceeds the expected return on attention. The citizen withdraws; so, eventually, does the elite." },
22	  { num: "V",  title: "Economic Revolution",
23	    seed: "What comes next, and how we avoid the failure modes of the last one.",
24	    body: "Revolution requires a coherent alternative, not merely a critique. The alternative must be operable by humans of ordinary virtue, or it will be captured by humans of extraordinary ambition." },
25	];
26	export default pillars;
27

1	// Prose narrative for the PRP project page.
2	// Sources (every claim grounded in existing repo content):
3	//   - preface.ts / preface.mdx → the refusal, the confession, "metric is the
4	//                                meaning", "the symptoms are not accidental"
5	//   - pillars.ts (I–V)         → corporatism, gamification, incumbency,
6	//                                 complacency, economic revolution
7	//   - arc.ts                   → five-volume arc; Book I status: writing
8	//   - progress.ts              → 92,143 / 100,000 words, manuscript v4
9	//   - quotes.ts                → severance, leaderboard-as-workflow,
10	//                                 incumbency wired into the substrate
11	// Per v2 principle 2 (content first), each section opens with its point.
12	
13	export type BodySection = {
14	  slug: string;
15	  heading: string;
16	  paragraphs: string[];
17	};
18	
19	const sections: BodySection[] = [
20	  {
21	    slug: "the-distinction",
22	    heading: "The distinction",
23	    paragraphs: [
24	      "Purposeless Efficiency is the name for a specific severance: efficiency cut loose from any external purpose, until the metric is the meaning. The book’s opening line is the cleanest statement of the argument — efficiency, severed from purpose, becomes the most efficient way of arriving nowhere. The distinction is not between efficient and inefficient systems. It is between systems that still remember what they are for and systems that have replaced their purpose with the measurement of themselves.",
25	      "The book’s claim is that this severance is not a local pathology of one industry or one institution. It is the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and the symptoms — the sense that the levers no longer connect to outcomes, the sense that the leaderboard has eaten the work — are not accidental.",
26	    ],
27	  },
28	  {
29	    slug: "why-it-matters",
30	    heading: "Why it matters",
31	    paragraphs: [
32	      "The reader, the preface assumes, has already noticed the symptoms. The book is written for the reader who has stopped pretending they are accidental. The wager is that naming the severance precisely — distinguishing optimization from purpose, metric from meaning — is a precondition for any honest response to it. Critique that misdescribes the disease prescribes the wrong medicine.",
33	      "The stakes are not aesthetic. When every workflow becomes a leaderboard, the leaderboard becomes the workflow, and the optimization target eats the optimized object. The institution survives; the thing it was supposed to do does not. Ambition, under these conditions, becomes the willingness to mistake the institution’s survival for one’s own. That substitution is what the book is trying to make visible enough to refuse.",
34	    ],
35	  },
36	  {
37	    slug: "where-it-shows-up",
38	    heading: "Where it shows up",
39	    paragraphs: [
40	      "The manuscript organizes the symptoms into five pillars. The first is corporatism: the management of human attention by institutions whose primary product is no longer goods or services but the legitimation of their own continued existence. The second is gamification: when every workflow becomes a leaderboard, the leaderboard becomes the workflow; the optimization target eats the optimized object, and the worldview follows.",
41	      "The third pillar is incumbency. Incumbents persist not because they are right but because they are wired into the substrate of decision-making; their replacement requires not better arguments but a different substrate. The fourth is complacency: the rational response to a system whose levers have been disconnected from outcomes. The cost of paying attention exceeds the expected return on attention; the citizen withdraws, and so, eventually, does the elite.",
42	      "The fifth pillar turns from diagnosis to prescription: the shape of an economic revolution that does not repeat the failure modes of the last one. Revolution, the book argues, requires a coherent alternative rather than a critique. The alternative must be operable by humans of ordinary virtue, or it will be captured by humans of extraordinary ambition.",
43	    ],
44	  },
45	  {
46	    slug: "what-the-manuscript-covers",
47	    heading: "What the manuscript covers",
48	    paragraphs: [
49	      "Book I is the diagnostic volume. It opens with a preface — the book began as a refusal, and as a confession, that I was good at producing inside systems whose only consistent output was the perpetuation of themselves — and walks the five pillars in turn, building the argument from corporatism through to the conditions under which an economic revolution would be honest rather than performative.",
50	      "Purposeless Efficiency is Book I of a five-volume series. The remaining volumes treat what comes after the severance: the institutions that survive it, the ones that do not, and what an honest economic order might look like once we admit the mistake. Working titles for Books II through V are provisional; the volumes are in planning, not drafting.",
51	    ],
52	  },
53	  {
54	    slug: "status",
55	    heading: "Status",
56	    paragraphs: [
57	      "Book I is in active writing on manuscript version four, at roughly 92,000 of a target 100,000 words. Books II through V are scoped at the volume level and remain in planning with provisional titles. There is no public release date and no publisher announcement; the page exists to make the argument legible while the manuscript continues. Readers who want to be notified when the book is ready can use the notify list on the project record.",
58	    ],
59	  },
60	];
61	
62	export default sections;
63

1	// TODO(michael): rewrite in your voice; keep under 240 words
2	const methodology = [
3	  "Theseus extracts first principles from discussions, uploaded essays, and interviews using a language model fine-tuned on Michael's own working corpus. Each principle is stored with its provenance — the source text and the moment of extraction.",
4	  "Principles are linked into a graph of supports and tensions. A second model — Noosphere — walks this graph at intervals, flagging contradictions, asking refining questions, and proposing edits to bring sibling principles into coherence.",
5	  "The system is not a chatbot. It is a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.",
6	];
7	export default methodology;
8

1	// Seed dataset. TODO(michael): replace with the real principle set
2	// extracted from thesescodex.com once the API is available.
3	
4	export type Principle = {
5	  id: string;          // P-014
6	  text: string;
7	  supports: string[];  // ids it supports
8	  tensions: string[];  // ids it tenses with
9	};
10	
11	const principles: Principle[] = [
12	  { id: "P-001", text: "Knowledge is provisional; revision is a feature, not a flaw.", supports: ["P-002","P-022"], tensions: [] },
13	  { id: "P-002", text: "Evidence is weighed, not counted.", supports: ["P-014"], tensions: [] },
14	  { id: "P-014", text: "Autonomy is prior to welfare.", supports: ["P-015"], tensions: ["P-071"] },
15	  { id: "P-015", text: "Consent is the operator of legitimacy.", supports: [], tensions: ["P-071"] },
16	  { id: "P-022", text: "Preferences over outcomes are revealed through choice under constraint.", supports: ["P-002"], tensions: ["P-031"] },
17	  { id: "P-031", text: "Stated preferences and revealed preferences diverge predictably.", supports: [], tensions: ["P-022"] },
18	  { id: "P-040", text: "Power flows toward whatever is measured.", supports: ["P-052"], tensions: [] },
19	  { id: "P-052", text: "Metrics, once made consequential, cease to be informative.", supports: [], tensions: [] },
20	  { id: "P-061", text: "Institutions outlive the problems they were designed to solve.", supports: ["P-070"], tensions: [] },
21	  { id: "P-070", text: "Replacement is harder than reform; reform is harder than capture.", supports: [], tensions: [] },
22	  { id: "P-071", text: "Collective survival overrides individual consent in extremis.", supports: [], tensions: ["P-014","P-015"] },
23	  { id: "P-080", text: "A model that cannot be wrong cannot be useful.", supports: ["P-001"], tensions: [] },
24	];
25	
26	export default principles;
27

1	// Prose narrative for the THS project page.
2	// Sources (every named principle and named example grounded in repo content):
3	//   - thesis.ts         → claim, method, role
4	//   - methodology.ts    → extraction with provenance; Noosphere walking the
5	//                          graph at intervals; not-a-chatbot framing
6	//   - principles.ts     → P-001, P-002, P-014, P-015, P-022, P-031, P-040,
7	//                          P-052, P-061, P-070, P-071, P-080 with their
8	//                          stated supports / tensions
9	//   - noosphere-feed.ts → the three feed kinds (DETECTED, OPEN QUESTION,
10	//                          REFINED) and the worked example of P-014 vs P-071
11	// The prompt asks the page to answer "what does the Theseus project argue
12	// about identity?". Theseus does not argue a substantive claim about
13	// identity; it argues that the question of which commitments survive
14	// revision is decidable by structure, and builds the system to make that
15	// structure visible. The body holds that line.
16	
17	export type BodySection = {
18	  slug: string;
19	  heading: string;
20	  paragraphs: string[];
21	};
22	
23	const sections: BodySection[] = [
24	  {
25	    slug: "the-ship-of-theseus-briefly",
26	    heading: "The Ship of Theseus, briefly",
27	    paragraphs: [
28	      "The puzzle in the name is the old one: a ship has each of its planks replaced over time, and at the end of the process there is a question about whether it is still the same ship. The puzzle has had a long career as a thought experiment about identity, but the project does not borrow it for the metaphysics. It borrows it for the method. The interesting question is not whether the ship is the same ship; it is which planks, when swapped, force you to admit that something has changed.",
29	      "Theseus, the project, is a knowledge system for keeping that ledger honestly. It extracts first principles from discussion and writing, links them into a graph of supports and tensions, and runs a second model — Noosphere — across the graph to flag contradictions, ask refining questions, and propose edits. The thesis is plain: philosophical debate can be systematized. The role is a Kim or a Codex for the operator-philosopher.",
30	    ],
31	  },
32	  {
33	    slug: "three-orders-of-identity-change",
34	    heading: "Three orders of identity change",
35	    paragraphs: [
36	      "The system tracks three orders of change to a principle, and each is a different answer to the Ship-of-Theseus question. The kinds come straight off the Noosphere feed and do most of the work the page is trying to make visible.",
37	      "First, DETECTED. Two principles you have already committed to are flagged as in tension — Noosphere notices, for example, that P-014 (\"autonomy is prior to welfare\") tenses with P-071 (\"collective survival overrides individual consent in extremis\"). Nothing has been replaced yet. The ship is intact, and an inspector has tapped one of the planks and said: this one, here, is not what you think it is.",
38	      "Second, OPEN QUESTION. The contradiction is not silently absorbed. It is rendered as a question that has to be answered before the graph is allowed to settle: under what conditions does P-014 yield to P-071, and does that yield imply a new meta-principle? The system refuses to choose for you. It only insists that the choice exists.",
39	      "Third, REFINED. A principle is rewritten in light of pressure from its neighbours — P-022, for instance, becomes \"preferences over outcomes are revealed through choice under constraint.\" The plank is replaced. The provenance of the old version is kept. Whether you call the resulting ship the same ship is your problem; whether the rewrite is consistent with the rest of the graph is the system's.",
40	    ],
41	  },
42	  {
43	    slug: "severity",
44	    heading: "Severity",
45	    paragraphs: [
46	      "Severity is the question of how much a contradiction is allowed to cost before it forces a change. Theseus reads it off the structure of the graph rather than off the heat of the argument. A tension between two principles that each support nothing else is a local quarrel. A tension whose endpoints are load-bearing — P-014 supports P-015 (\"consent is the operator of legitimacy\"), and both of them sit in tension with P-071 — propagates. The replacement of a plank near the keel is not the same as the replacement of a plank on the deck.",
47	      "The seed dataset deliberately includes a few of these load-bearing edges so the severity reading has something to grade. P-022 (\"preferences over outcomes are revealed through choice under constraint\") and P-031 (\"stated preferences and revealed preferences diverge predictably\") are stated as a tension on purpose: they are both true in their domains, and Theseus is meant to surface that the cost of holding both is the requirement to specify which domain you are in when you appeal to either.",
48	    ],
49	  },
50	  {
51	    slug: "refusal-cases",
52	    heading: "Refusal cases",
53	    paragraphs: [
54	      "Theseus is allowed, and required, to refuse. The refusal cases are the principles whose function in the graph is to bound what the system itself is willing to do. P-080 (\"a model that cannot be wrong cannot be useful\") is the cleanest example: it forbids any version of Theseus that quietly hides its own contradictions in order to look coherent. P-001 (\"knowledge is provisional; revision is a feature, not a flaw\") sits behind it and refuses, in the other direction, the temptation to treat any current state of the graph as final.",
55	      "The OPEN QUESTION feed kind is the operational shape of refusal. When a contradiction is detected and no resolution is yet defensible, Noosphere does not pick. It writes the question and waits. The slow, structured collaborator described in the methodology depends on this — it is what distinguishes the system from a chatbot that always has a take.",
56	    ],
57	  },
58	  {
59	    slug: "fixed-points",
60	    heading: "Fixed points",
61	    paragraphs: [
62	      "Fixed points are the principles a graph keeps coming back to under revision — the planks that survive every pass of the inspector. In the seed dataset they are the ones with no recorded tensions: P-001 on revisability, P-002 (\"evidence is weighed, not counted\"), P-040 (\"power flows toward whatever is measured\"), P-052 (\"metrics, once made consequential, cease to be informative\"), P-061 (\"institutions outlive the problems they were designed to solve\"), and P-070 (\"replacement is harder than reform; reform is harder than capture\"). Their stability is not a guarantee that they are right; it is a record that they have not yet been forced into a contradiction by anything else in the graph.",
63	      "Fixed points are the project's answer to the original puzzle. Identity, for Theseus, is not the persistence of the planks but the persistence of the constraints the planks have to satisfy. A position changes when a fixed point falls. Until then, the ship is still the ship.",
64	    ],
65	  },
66	  {
67	    slug: "status",
68	    heading: "Status",
69	    paragraphs: [
70	      "Theseus is live. The current seed dataset is twelve principles, with provenance carried alongside each one and a small Noosphere feed of detections, open questions, and refinements. The intended next step is to replace the seed set with the working corpus extracted from thesescodex.com once that API is available. The page exists to make the argument legible while the dataset grows; the system itself is the argument.",
71	    ],
72	  },
73	];
74	
75	export default sections;
76

input.content:
import type { Project, ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "hivemind",
  code: "HVM",
  title: "Hivemind",
  tagline:
    "Strategic analytical software for groups making consequential decisions.",
  kind: "company",
  status: "active",
  startedISO: "2024-06-01",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-12",
  stage: "Seed",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/hivemind",
  // TODO(michael): supply real links
  links: [
    { label: "hivemind.ai",            href: "https://hivemind.ai",            external: true },
    { label: "app.hivemind.ai",        href: "https://app.hivemind.ai",        external: true },
    { label: "michael@hivemind.ai",    href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai" },
  ],
  summary:
    "Hivemind is software that helps teams reason through hard strategic decisions. It breaks a question into testable sub-claims, attaches weighted evidence to each, and rolls the result up into a structured brief — citations, confidence intervals, dissents — that a team can argue with and update without redoing the work. It exists because the memos behind consequential decisions usually hide their assumptions; a graph that lays them out is one a team can attack.",
};

export const meta: Pick<
  ProjectMetadata,
  "slug" | "code" | "title" | "tagline" | "framing" | "status"
> = {
  slug: project.slug,
  code: project.code,
  title: project.title,
  tagline: project.tagline,
  framing: project.summary,
  status: "in-progress",
};

export default project;

input.content:
import type { Project, ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "purposeless-efficiency",
  code: "PRP",
  title: "Purposeless Efficiency",
  tagline: "Book I — what happens when efficiency forgets what it was for.",
  kind: "book",
  status: "draft",
  startedISO: "2024-01-15",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-12",
  stage: "manuscript · MS v4",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/purposeless-efficiency",
  links: [
    { label: "manuscript page", href: "/purposeless-efficiency" },
    { label: "notify list",     href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai?subject=PRP%20%2F%20NOTIFY" },
  ],
  summary:
    "Purposeless Efficiency is a book about what happens when optimization gets cut loose from any examined purpose — when the metric becomes the meaning and institutions forget what they were for. It traces the consequences across five pillars (corporatism, gamification, incumbency, complacency, and the shape of an honest economic revolution), arguing that the sense that the levers no longer connect to outcomes is structural, not accidental. Book I of a five-volume series, written for readers who have stopped pretending the symptoms are accidents.",
  citation: "Quintin, M. (forthcoming). Purposeless Efficiency. Book I of a five-volume series.",
};

export const meta: Pick<
  ProjectMetadata,
  "slug" | "code" | "title" | "tagline" | "framing" | "status"
> = {
  slug: project.slug,
  code: project.code,
  title: project.title,
  tagline: project.tagline,
  framing: project.summary,
  status: "in-progress",
};

export default project;

import type { Project, ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";

export const project: Project = {
  slug: "theseus",
  code: "THS",
  title: "Theseus",
  tagline:
    "A knowledge system that flags contradictions between your stated principles.",
  kind: "research",
  status: "active",
  startedISO: "2024-09-01",
  updatedISO: "2026-04-19",
  stage: "live",
  classification: "public",
  authors: ["Michael Quintin"],
  customPage: "/theseus",
  links: [
    { label: "thesescodex.com", href: "https://thesescodex.com", external: true },
    { label: "michael@hivemind.ai", href: "mailto:michael@hivemind.ai" },
  ],
  summary:
    "Theseus is software that takes the principles you have stated in writing and conversation, links them into a graph of supports and tensions, and flags where they contradict each other. The product is a slow, structured collaborator — not a chatbot — for anyone who needs to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves. It is built for the operator-philosopher who would rather argue with their own positions before someone else does.",
};

export const meta: Pick<
  ProjectMetadata,
  "slug" | "code" | "title" | "tagline" | "framing" | "status"
> = {
  slug: project.slug,
  code: project.code,
  title: project.title,
  tagline: project.tagline,
  framing: project.summary,
  status: "shipped",
};

export default project;

It breaks a question into testable sub-claims, attaches weighted evidence to each, and rolls the result up into a structured brief — citations, confidence intervals, dissents — that a team can argue with and update without redoing the work. It exists because the memos behind consequential decisions usually hide their assumptions; a graph that lays them out is one a team can attack.",

    "Theseus extracts first principles from discussion and writing, links them into a graph of supports and tensions, and runs a second model — Noosphere — across the graph to flag contradictions, ask refining questions, and propose edits. The product is a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves. The output is a contradiction map with provenance, not a debate.",

content.text:
All checks pass. Summary of changes:

**Top-level project files** (`src/content/projects/{hivemind,purposeless-efficiency,theseus}.ts`):
- New cold-read taglines (all ≤ 80 chars: 72 / 62 / 76)
- Refreshed `summary` to act as 2-3 sentence framing (what + why + contribution)
- Added `export const meta` with `{slug, code, title, tagline, framing, status}` per B1

**Sub-folder metadata files** (`src/content/projects/<slug>/metadata.ts`):
- Aligned `tagline` and `framing` so the v2 project page (`ProjectShell` reads `metadata.tagline`/`metadata.framing`) renders the new copy

**Sub-files reviewed** (B4):
- All 9 sub-files (thesis/capabilities/workflow, preface/pillars/arc, thesis/methodology/principles) are well under 600 words (max 296)
- Content remains internally consistent with the new framing — none were deleted (B non-goal preserved)

**Verification**:
- Required files all present
- `npm run lint`: passes (only pre-existing `_legacy_v1` warnings, unrelated)
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: clean
- Tagline character counts: HVM 72, PRP 62, THS 76 — all ≤ 80
